citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
Housekeeping at the facility needs to be addressed. There are old feces left in empty enclosures that have not been
cleaned and removed. There is excess feces and shavings underneath a section of whelping boxes not in use with a dead
mouse on the floor and rodent droppings throughout the area. Excess feces left and not cleaned in the area can
contribute to a smell in the kennel. These areas of the kennel must be kept clean and in good repair to facilitate good
husbandry practices.
Correct by: March 15, 2026

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
The facility does not have an effective method of pest management in the kennel. There was evidence of mice via
droppings all around the food storage area and along the walls in the kennel by the whelping boxes and in the empty dirty
enclosures. Pest management must be addressed to reduce and control the number of rodents in the area to protect the
food supplies from potential contamination and to reduce risk to the dogs.
Correct by: March 18, 2026

The last complete physical exam done on the dogs from head to tail was performed on Jan 15, 2025. This exam is to be
done by the attending veterinarian not less than once every 12 months. The licensee needs to contact the vet and
schedule this exam to meet the requirements of the regulation and to assure that the dogs are examined to note any
changes in health that may need to be addressed.
Correct by: April 13, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
3.11(c) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
Housekeeping at the facility needs to be addressed. There are old feces left in empty enclosures that have not been 
cleaned and removed. There is excess feces and shavings underneath a section of whelping boxes not in use with a dead 
mouse on the floor and rodent droppings throughout the area. Excess feces left and not cleaned in the area can 
contribute to a smell in the kennel. These areas of the kennel must be kept clean and in good repair to facilitate good 
husbandry practices.  
Correct by: March 15, 2026 
3.11(d) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
The facility does not have an effective method of pest management in the kennel. There was evidence of mice via 
droppings all around the food storage area and along the walls in the kennel by the whelping boxes and in the empty dirty 
enclosures. Pest management must be addressed to reduce and control the number of rodents in the area to protect the 
food supplies from potential contamination and to reduce risk to the dogs.  
Correct by: March 18, 2026 
3.13(a)(2) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The last complete physical exam done on the dogs from head to tail was performed on Jan 15, 2025. This exam is to be 
done by the attending veterinarian not less than once every 12 months. The licensee needs to contact the vet and 
schedule this exam to meet the requirements of the regulation and to assure that the dogs are examined to note any 
changes in health that may need to be addressed.  
Correct by: April 13, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
